execution_summary: End-to-end build for local-todo-sqlite completed per pipeline: Discovery decision build, architecture-brief.json v1 aligned, delivery registry with architecture_refs (including quality-gate / qa-validation), engineering manager executed 01-next-bun-scaffold through 06-todo-frontend-ui then quality-gate with correct agent+sector, EM engineering_execution_report.validation_status pass (bun run lint and bun run test exit 0, 38 tests), and all seven task.json files show status done with ownership populated.
what_worked_well: Quality gate registered as a normal task_slug with qa-agent / quality-engineering and depends_on spanning all implementation tasks, matching production-workflow delivery contract.
Linear depends_on chain from scaffold through server boundary to UI reduced cross-task ambiguity and matched clean layering in the brief.
Task acceptance criteria remained testable and traceable to architecture-brief constraints (singleton policy, boundary validation, Atomic Design).
Agent permissions table in agent-permissions-boundaries.mdc aligns with orchestrator invocation boundary, lowering bypass risk during the run.
issues_identified: Plan filenames using strict ISO-8601 with `:` (time portion) are not creatable on Windows filesystems; on-disk plan name uses a colon-free UTC timestamp while `created_at` stays ISO-8601 Z in JSON.
Exploration was re-run in-thread without an explicit dedupe pointer (Deltas only / unchanged), increasing token cost and risking divergent options versus the first pass.

Five consecutive backend-engineering assignments before the single frontend task concentrates sequential work and delays UI-visible feedback until late in EM execution (valid under one-sector-per-task but operationally heavy).
Registry task.json remains minimal by design, so structured QA evidence (command logs, checklist outcomes) lives only in EM narrative unless EM standardizes where it lands.
root_causes: mandatory-exploration.mdc allows quoting prior exploration for the same user request, but orchestrator handoffs did not enforce a compact continuation contract when Exploration was invoked again mid-pipeline.
Delivery sliced tasks primarily by architectural layer (domain, application, infra, adapters) rather than thin vertical increments, which naturally serializes backend work.
No repo-wide convention (outside task.json) for attaching machine-readable QA artifacts to a feature folder without expanding the registry schema.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Reduces redundant Exploration passes and keeps options stable when the orchestrator continues the same build thread.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: Edit `.cursor/rules/mandatory-exploration.mdc`: add a short subsection for orchestrator-led pipeline threads requiring that a re-invoked `exploration-agent` receive either (a) verbatim prior Output Requirements sections plus `Exploration: unchanged (see above)` from the orchestrator, or (b) an explicit `Deltas only` delta list; forbid a blank-slate re-run when a complete exploration for the same objective is already in the thread. Mirror one orchestrator.md bullet under Mandatory precursor telling the orchestrator to attach prior exploration or deltas when re-invoking Step 2 in the same thread.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Binds Step 2 validation to the mandatory-exploration dedupe behavior so orchestrator retries are checklist-driven.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: Edit `.cursor/skills/production-workflow/SKILL.md` Step 2 (Invoke Exploration): add one validation bullet stating that if validated exploration output for the same problem statement is already present in the thread, the orchestrator must either skip re-invocation per policy or re-invoke with an explicit delta-only payload and reject generic full repeats without anchoring text.

improvement_opportunities.rationale: Speeds future delivery-agent registration of QA tasks and reinforces architecture_refs including qa-validation.
improvement_opportunities.proposed_change: Add `tasks/_template/quality-gate.task.json` as a copy-ready stub mirroring the quality-gate registry shape (depends_on left as TODO array, acceptance focused on documented verify commands + structured QA handoff).

non_apply_recommendations.rationale: When UX feedback latency matters, pure layer-by-layer tasks increase time-to-first-visible increment.
non_apply_recommendations.proposed_change: For future features, interleave thin UI-facing slices with backend tasks where dependencies allow, or add an explicit early UI shell task that does not violate one-sector-per-task.

non_apply_recommendations.rationale: Improves auditability without expanding task.json schema.
non_apply_recommendations.proposed_change: Standardize engineering_execution_report.artifacts.paths to always include pointers to any saved QA logs or checklists when qa-agent produces files.

non_apply_recommendations.rationale: Optional hard anchor for dedupe across sessions.
non_apply_recommendations.proposed_change: If runs span multiple chats, store exploration JSON or markdown under a team-approved location and reference it from orchestrator Step 2 inputs (coordinate with repo policy; paths outside Step 11 allowlist are not orchestrator-applied automatically).
